producer: demiurge MONDALOY.md §9.7 — wet-lab handoff prep for demiurge-SX500-RE-c1 (cockpit gate-product: composition table + single-crystal/cast growth protocol stub + reproducibility checklist)
purpose: Cockpit-gate handoff packet — the non-wet-lab deliverable immediately preceding the wet-lab boundary (d1). Bundles (a) the c1 composition table in the form a melter / single-crystal grower / AM operator consumes, (b) two synthesis protocol stubs (investment-cast polycrystalline coupon AND LPBF additive-manufactured coupon — c1 is alumina-forming + printability-capped, both routes are in scope), (c) a reproducibility checklist a third-party lab must complete to make a measured-oracle PASS possible later. Nothing here measures c1; this is the manifest a wet-lab partner uses to attempt the cast/print.

boundary.what_this_record_is: A pre-wet-lab handoff packet. simulation-only-prediction holds — the composition is from §9.6 analytical funnel, the protocols are stubs derived from public Ni-superalloy practice.
boundary.what_this_record_is_NOT: Not a measured-oracle record. Not a fabrication report. Not a property measurement. absorbed stays false. provisional=true reflects that the protocol stubs need a melter/printer-shop review before any actual heat is melted.
boundary.wet_lab_boundary: Any subsequent measured-oracle record (oxidation k_p, γ′ vol fraction by SEM/SAXS, creep life) must be filed as a SEPARATE exports/ record and only THEN may absorbed flip — and only on the analog reference (Haynes-214 / René-N5 class), never on SX500 itself (d6 — SX500 has no measured oracle accessible to demiurge).

Al.role: α-Al2O3 former — THE ox-rich lever (Giggins-Pettit region III)

Hf.role: reactive element — α-Al2O3 scale adhesion (René N5 precedent 0.2)

composition_table_target_at_pct_derived.note: atomic-percent re-expression of the wt% table for melt-shop charge calc and DFT/CALPHAD downstream input (atomic masses g/mol: Ni 58.69, Cr 52.00, Al 26.98, Co 58.93, Mo 95.96, Ti 47.87, Hf 178.49, Y 88.91, C 12.01, B 10.81). Values rounded to 0.01 at%.
composition_table_target_at_pct_derived.rounding_note: Sum closes to 100.00 within rounding (computed via n_i = wt_i / M_i, normalised). DFT/CALPHAD downstream should use full-precision moles, not rounded at%.

raw_material_purity_minima.Al: 99.99 % (4N — reactive-element-effect requires low S, P, O)
raw_material_purity_minima.Ti: Grade-2 sponge or higher, low O (<0.1 wt%)
raw_material_purity_minima.Hf: 99.9 % (Zr-stripped; Hf/Zr separation must be done — bulk Hf often Zr-contaminated)
raw_material_purity_minima.B: amorphous B 99.9 % or B4C master alloy
raw_material_purity_minima.rationale: S poisons the reactive-element effect (Hf/Y getter S; if S is high the α-Al2O3 scale spalls). O above 30 wppm pre-oxidises Al during melt. Both are well-known alumina-forming-superalloy lessons (Reed 2006 ch.4).

route_A_investment_cast_polycrystalline_.purpose: First-screen route — fastest path to an oxidation-test coupon. Polycrystalline equiaxed; lower mechanical-property bar but valid for scale-formation studies.
steps: 1. Vacuum induction melting (VIM) — argon/vacuum atmosphere <1e-3 mbar, melt all elements except Y, Hf, B at 1550-1600 C.
2. Add Hf, Y, B as last addition (reactive elements oxidise fastest — minimise dissolved time).
3. Pour at ~1500 C into a preheated investment-cast shell (mullite/zircon face coat); coupon geometry: 20 mm × 20 mm × 3 mm oxidation-test discs (≥6 per heat for statistics).
4. Solution heat treat 1250 C / 4 h / Ar, gas-fan-cool to room T.
5. Two-step aging: 1080 C / 4 h / Ar fan-cool + 870 C / 16 h / Ar fan-cool — standard alumina-former γ′-precipitation cycle (Reed 2006).
6. Surface prep: grind to 600-grit SiC, then 1-μm diamond polish; ultrasonic-clean in acetone + ethanol.
7. Heat-tint visual check before oxidation: bright metallic, no surface oxide islands.
route_A_investment_cast_polycrystalline_.deliverables_to_lab: ≥6 coupons + chemistry-of-record (ICP-OES on a sacrificial slug from the heat) + heat-treatment trace.
route_A_investment_cast_polycrystalline_.estimated_first_heat_time: 2-3 weeks at a university melt-shop (e.g. Cambridge Rolls-Royce UTC, Ohio State CHTM)

route_B_LPBF_additive_manufactured_coupo.purpose: Second route — exercises the SX500 'castable AND printable' public spec; confirms the c1 γ′ window stays inside the LPBF printability envelope (the §9.6 Al+Ti=6.5 wt% / 35-40 % γ′ rule).
steps: 1. Gas-atomise the c1 charge to 15-45 μm powder (EIGA or VIGA, Ar-protected); sieve, dry, store under Ar.
2. LPBF on EOS M290 / Concept-Laser M2 / equivalent — preheat plate 200 C, Ar atmosphere O2 <50 ppm.
3. Parameter sweep (Vd × P matrix) — laser power 200-350 W, scan speed 800-1400 mm/s, layer 30 μm, hatch 110 μm; build 4 coupons per (P, v) cell, 5 cells = 20 coupons.
4. CT-scan all 20 builds for cracks/porosity; downselect to the parameter cell with porosity <0.05 % and zero hot-cracks.
5. HIP at 1180 C / 100 MPa / 4 h to close residual porosity.
6. Solution + age per route A steps 4-5.
7. Same surface prep as route A.
route_B_LPBF_additive_manufactured_coupo.deliverables_to_lab: Downselected parameter set + ≥6 HIPed/heat-treated coupons + powder-batch chemistry + CT report.
route_B_LPBF_additive_manufactured_coupo.estimated_first_heat_time: 4-6 weeks (atomisation 2 wk + parameter sweep 2 wk + post-process 1-2 wk)
route_B_LPBF_additive_manufactured_coupo.risk_note: Hot-cracking probability is the c1 unknown — if the (Al+Ti) loading drives cracks, the §9.6 funnel must be re-run with a lower γ′ target (Al 4.0, Ti 1.0).

oxidation_kinetics_isothermal_TGA.purpose: Measure parabolic k_p in the α-Al2O3 regime to compare against the §9.6 estimate k_p ~1e-13 to 1e-12 g^2 cm^-4 s^-1.
oxidation_kinetics_isothermal_TGA.protocol: Thermogravimetric analysis, 1100 C, dry air (and separately wet air with 10 % H2O for the OH-volatility check), 200 h, continuous Δm logging; post-test SEM-EDS cross-section for scale identification (α-Al2O3 vs Cr2O3 vs mixed).

gamma_prime_volume_fraction.protocol: Aqua-regia + HCl electrolytic etch + SEM image analysis on ≥10 fields; cross-check via small-angle X-ray scattering (SAXS) if available.

scale_adhesion_thermal_cycling.purpose: Test the reactive-element effect (Hf+Y) under thermal cycling — the historical alumina-former failure mode is scale spallation, not scale growth.
scale_adhesion_thermal_cycling.protocol: 1100 C ↔ 100 C cycling, 1-h holds, 100 cycles; mass-change logging + post-test SEM for spallation fraction.

phase_identification.purpose: Rule out σ / μ TCP phases (PHACOMP gate confirmation).
phase_identification.protocol: XRD + SEM-EBSD on aged coupon after 200 h / 900 C exposure (TCP precipitation temperature window).

reproducibility_checklist: [ ] Chemistry-of-record: ICP-OES on sacrificial heat slug, all 10 elements + S/P/O impurities, within tolerance band above.
[ ] Heat-treatment trace: time-temperature log archived (csv) for solution + 2-step age.
[ ] Coupon geometry + count documented; ≥6 coupons per condition for statistics.
[ ] Surface-prep procedure pinned (grit sequence, final polish, cleaning solvent, time-before-test).
[ ] TGA: instrument calibration with sapphire blank, repeat baseline before each run.
[ ] Oxidation atmosphere flow rate + dew-point recorded for every TGA run.
[ ] All raw data + analysis scripts deposited in an open repo (Zenodo / OSF) with the candidate_id 'demiurge-SX500-RE-c1' in metadata.
[ ] Independent re-melt by a second lab from the same composition spec before any 'verified' claim (analog precedent: H3S superconductivity took 3 independent groups before community accepted).
[ ] If absorbed=true is to be claimed on the ANALOG reference (Haynes-214 or René-N5-class) — a separate analog-validation record must be filed first, citing the same protocol.
[ ] SX500 itself remains absorbed=false PERMANENTLY in demiurge (d6) — no SX500-direct measured oracle is accessible; c1 is the open functionally-equivalent candidate, not SX500.

wet_lab_partner_options.academic_first_choice: alumina-forming-superalloy groups: Cambridge (Rolls-Royce UTC), Ohio State (CHTM), Imperial College, MPIE Düsseldorf — all have alumina-formers as active programs.
wet_lab_partner_options.industrial_handoff: Haynes International / Special Metals / Cannon-Muskegon — commercial melt-shops that routinely cast alumina-forming Ni superalloys.
wet_lab_partner_options.additive_specialist: Oak Ridge MDF or Fraunhofer ILT for the LPBF parameter sweep.
wet_lab_partner_options.honest_caveat: demiurge does not commission wet-lab work — this handoff packet is the open-source artefact a partner can pick up. No demiurge guarantee of fabrication, only of the design rules.

honest_observations: Protocol stubs are pinned to public alumina-forming-superalloy practice (Reed 2006, Pollock review 2006, Haynes 214 datasheet). They are NOT proprietary SX500 protocols — c1 is open, the cast/print routes are open.

First wet-lab failure mode prediction: LPBF hot-cracking (route B) is the most likely first hurdle — if it cracks, the §9.6 funnel can be re-run with lower (Al+Ti) loading. This is the d2-style breakthrough path on the printability wall.
absorbed=false / provisional=true / simulation-only-prediction — this is a handoff packet, not a measurement. No d6 violation; no SX500 claim.

The d6 absolute boundary: even a successful c1 cast + measured k_p in line with prediction would only let demiurge flip absorbed=true on the ANALOG c1 measurement record — never on SX500. SX500's absorbed stays false permanently in demiurge.
next_steps_after_this_handoff: Find a wet-lab partner (academic alumina-forming-superalloy group preferred) willing to cast/print a c1 coupon set.

In parallel, demiurge can still drive the non-wet-lab pipeline further: pycalphad + open Ni-Cr-Al-Co TDB phase-equilibrium run (numerical γ′ fraction confirmation); DFT α-Al2O3 / Ni-substrate adhesion-energy check; physics-descriptor ML creep estimate (arxiv:2212.06755).

If a partner runs route A, file the measurement as a new exports/ record (kind = 'analog_oxidation_measured_oracle'); only THEN consider an absorbed=true flip on that analog record (NOT on c1's design record, NOT on SX500).
If hot-cracking blocks route B, re-execute §9.6 with (Al=4.0, Ti=1.0) → candidate c2 — d2 breakthrough path on the printability wall.
r4_invariant: absorbed=false · gate_type=simulation-only-prediction · provisional=true · domain=material · verb=handoff. D1 clean-room satisfied (all protocol stubs derived from public superalloy practice; no proprietary SX500 process disclosed). d6 boundary held (SX500 absorbed=false permanent; analog measurements would be filed as separate records). d2 preserved (printability/hot-cracking risk surfaced with concrete c2-fallback breakthrough path, not concession).
citations: Reed, R.C. 'The Superalloys: Fundamentals and Applications' Cambridge UP 2006 — heat-treatment + reactive-element + alumina-former protocols
Pollock, T.M. & Tin, S. 2006 J. Propul. Power 22:361 — Ni-base single-crystal superalloys review (impurity limits, casting practice)
Haynes 214 datasheet (Haynes International) — public alumina-forming Ni superalloy analog reference
Giggins & Pettit 1971 J. Electrochem. Soc. 118:1782 — Ni-Cr-Al oxidation map (the §9.6 region-III gate)
